evidence_summary: "Electrooxidation of thiophene in such media requires potentials above 1.6 V versus SCE. The high potentials (above 1.45–1.55 V) would irreversibly damage the conjugated system, due to overoxidation" → poor/degraded film
BFEE "lowered the oxidation potential of the monomer to about +0.9 V vs. SCE instead of… +1.6 V vs. SCE found in acetonitrile"; homogeneous flexible PT film
At low E only a "yellow haze" with negligible film; at high E non-uniform, non-electroactive brown deposit; uniform PT "cannot be electrodeposited" on tin oxide
Irreversible Eox ~1.75 V vs Ag/Ag⁺; very high potential → irreversible overoxidation; ring-opening products/electrolyte trapped; no quality conducting film in neutral MeCN
Lowered potential allows polyfuran film (low conductivity, ~10⁻² S/cm)
Irreversible Eox ~1.50 V; conductive poly(2-methylfuran) obtained (lower conductivity than polyfuran)
"This monomer proved to be impossible to electropolymerize. Therefore, a new trimer was designed… enclosed between two EDOT molecules"
Lower Eox than EDOT-CHO; trimer easily electropolymerized to film
Thick, electroactive, insoluble PDMOT film in MeCN (methoxy substitution still permits α-coupling)
"Electropolymerization of these disubstituted thiophenes was often unsuccessful, probably because of severe steric hindrance" blocking coupling
Oxidation onset 1.83 V vs SCE; high monomer Eox → overoxidation damages backbone, powdery/poor film in common solvent
Onset 1.23 V; "high-quality freestanding polyselenophene (PSe) films with conductivity as high as 2.8×10⁻¹ S/cm… first case for the preparation of high-quality PSe films"
Same-condition series: polythiophene from thiophene "showed almost no electrical conductivity" while bithiophene/terthiophene gave good films
Best-performing polythiophene film of the series (PBTP)
Low oxidation onset (~0.88 V); easy oxidative coupling to film
Poly(3-methylthiophene) film grown at ~1.3–1.4 V; well-known electroactive film
Eox ~1.1 V; fast adsorption, smooth flexible PEDOT film even at low monomer conc
PEDOT-type films readily grown in DCM/TBAPF₆
3,6-coupling → bicarbazyl → electroactive polycarbazole film
3,6 blocked; "dimerization of radical cations is slower (k_dim ≤ 1.5·10⁶ M⁻¹·s⁻¹) and no efficient electropolymerization occurs"; stable dimers don't deposit
No efficient film below 1.4 V; coupling only occurs "when potential was brought over 1.7 V/SCE because of formation of very reactive radicals… involving phenyl groups" (degradation)
"Very fast dimerization (k_dim = 1.5·10⁷ M⁻¹·s⁻¹) and electrochemical polymerization… leads to a thick adherent conducting polymer film"
Oxidizes then couples at 3,3′ to a discrete bicarbazyl dimer; does not propagate to a film
Radical-cation coupling; ~100% polymerization yield in MeCN; 2,7-linked polyfluorene film
Only ~15% polymerization yield in DCM vs ~100% in MeCN — solvent-dependent near-failure
Amino groups stabilize radical cation; reversible two-peak redox, electroactive film accumulates
Without acid, poor/insufficient film; "improvements… obtained by adding anhydrous acid to the medium" — neutral aprotic medium gives no quality PANI
Added anhydrous acid yields electroactive polyaniline film
Standard acidic aqueous polyaniline film growth
Radical cations undergo cation–cation coupling to diphenylbenzidine (soluble dimer), EC2EE mechanism; dimer is not a film (concentrated galvanostatic conditions can give a film)
"No polymer film formation observed on the substrate, even at times as long as 4 h"; only colored oligomer exudate; required DMF:MeCN 6:1 to deposit
Electron-donating amino group reduces radical-cation reactivity; soluble dimers/oligomers "diffuse into the solution instead of" depositing
1-bromopyrene/pyrene electropolymerize via 1,3,6,8 positions in BFEE to oligopyrene film (high Eox requires BFEE)
α,α′-disubstituted pyrroles give "a variety of soluble products which do not alter the electrode" — α–α′ coupling blocked
Low Eox (~0.8 V); classic adherent electroactive polypyrrole film

"Difficult to electropolymerize on the electrode… in conventional solvents such as acetonitrile (ACN), propylene carbonate (PC) and aqueous system"; high anthracene Eox + low solubility; works as copolymer with EDOT/thiophene/selenophene or as a 2.5 V solid device
